vocab.meaning: bắt đầu thực hiện việc gì đó một cách tự tin và hiệu quả nhất
vocab.example: It took a few matches for our football team to really get into our stride and start winning.

vocab.word: keep your eye on the prize
vocab.meaning: luôn tập trung vào mục tiêu cuối cùng
vocab.example: Being part of a competitive swimming relay taught me to keep my eye on the prize even when I was exhausted.

vocab.meaning: cùng nhau thành công hoặc cùng nhau thất bại
vocab.example: In a basketball squad, you sink or swim together; there's no room for individual egos.

vocab.meaning: gánh vác trọng trách cho cả đội
vocab.example: There were matches where our star player had to carry the team on his back to secure a victory.

vocab.meaning: một sân chơi công bằng cho tất cả mọi người
vocab.example: I loved school sports because they provided a level playing field where talent and hard work were the only things that mattered.

vocab.meaning: người dẫn đầu hoặc người có khả năng thắng cao nhất
vocab.example: Although we weren't the front-runners in the tournament, we managed to cause a major upset in the semi-finals.

vocab.meaning: hồi sức, tìm lại năng lượng để tiếp tục nỗ lực
vocab.example: Just as I was about to give up during the marathon relay, the cheering crowd helped me get a second wind.

vocab.meaning: toàn diện, trên mọi phương diện
vocab.example: The discipline I gained from being on a sports team improved my academic performance across the board.

vocab.meaning: vượt lên chính mình để xử lý tốt một tình huống khó khăn
vocab.example: In the final minutes of the championship, our goalkeeper really rose to the occasion and made some incredible saves.

vocab.meaning: thuận buồm xuôi gió, diễn ra suôn sẻ
vocab.example: Our journey to the finals wasn't all plain sailing; we had to overcome numerous injuries and setbacks.
